steps.title: Identify the passage's key claim about the data
steps.content: The passage states the data illustrated people's tendency to overlook the possibility of removing things. This means the graph must show that subtractive suggestions—those proposing removal—are dramatically fewer than additive ones, demonstrating a bias toward adding rather than removing.

steps.title: Match the clearest contrast to the claim
steps.content:
Choice C — fewer than 100 suggestions were coded as subtractive, whereas more than 550 suggestions were coded as additive. compares fewer than 100 subtractive suggestions directly against more than 550 additive suggestions, showing a stark disparity where people submit roughly 5–6 times more ideas for adding than for removing.

The other choices either compare irrelevant categories (neither/additive, invalid/subtractive) or misread the actual bar heights.

That matches Choice C — fewer than 100 suggestions were coded as subtractive, whereas more than 550 suggestions were coded as additive..
